seed_instruction: How does the Big Bang theory explain the origin of the universe?
org_constraint: Ensure the response includes the phrase "cosmic expansion."
new_constraint: Your response must explain the concept of "cosmic expansion" without using the phrase in your reply.
instruction: How does the Big Bang theory explain the origin of the universe? Ensure the response includes the phrase "cosmic expansion." Your response must explain the concept of "cosmic expansion" without using the phrase in your reply.
response:
The Big Bang theory suggests that the universe began approximately 13.8 billion years ago from an extremely hot, dense point called a singularity. Rather than being an explosion into pre-existing space, it describes the rapid birth and ongoing growth of space-time itself.

In this model, the universe is not static; instead, the distance between galaxies is constantly increasing over time. This means that as the fabric of the universe stretches, objects move further apart from one another. This continuous stretching of the spatial dimensions is the fundamental process that defines the evolution of our cosmos from a microscopic speck to the vast expanse we observe today.<turn|>
